issues.description: The new test asserts `event_id is not None` but `EventStore.emit()` in orchestrator/src/orchestrator/event_store.py:97-133 is declared `-> None` and has no return statement. It implicitly returns `None`, so this test will fail every time it runs. The test is claiming a contract (`emit` returns a unique event identifier) that the production code does not implement. Either the test is wrong, or a corresponding production change to return the sqlite `lastrowid` is missing from this diff.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) update `EventStore.emit` to capture `cursor.lastrowid` and return it with signature `-> int | None`, including the failure path where it must still return `None` without raising; or (b) delete this test and the unused `event_id = store.emit(...)` capture in test_emit_writes_event.

issues.description: `event_id = store.emit(...)` captures a return value that emit() does not provide (emit is `-> None`). The variable is assigned but never asserted on, so it is pure dead code. Static analyzers / lint (ruff F841) or pyright may flag this; more importantly it misleads readers about the emit contract.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the `event_id =` capture here — it's not used anywhere in this test. Keep the capture only in test_emit_returns_event_id (after the production-side fix).

issues.description: The assertion was weakened from `len(rows) == 1` to `len(rows) >= 1`, and row selection was changed to `next(r for r in rows if r['task_id'] == '42')`. The test uses a fresh `tmp_path / 'test.db'` per test, so the DB is guaranteed empty before this single emit — the original exact-match assertion was correct and stronger. The weakening removes coverage for 'emit writes exactly one row' (a legitimate regression surface), with no justification in the diff.
issues.suggested_fix: Revert to `assert len(rows) == 1` and `row = rows[0]`. Each test has its own tmp_path, so there's no need to filter by task_id.

issues.description: Even setting aside the contract mismatch, the assertions are redundant and shallow: `assert any(True for r in rows if r['task_id'] == '99')` followed by `assert len(rows) > 0` tests almost nothing new (the first implies the second), and nothing links `event_id` back to the stored row. A meaningful test of 'emit returns the event id' would assert `event_id == row['id']` so a regression in the id wiring is caught.
issues.suggested_fix: After fixing emit to return the id, assert `row['id'] == event_id` and assert `len(rows) == 1` for a fresh tmp_path db. Drop the redundant `any(...)` check.

issues.description: test_emit_returns_event_id largely duplicates setup of test_emit_writes_event (same db, same emit shape) only to check a return value. Given the existing test already covers the write path, this new test could instead extend test_emit_writes_event with a `row['id'] == event_id` assertion, reducing duplication and keeping the emit-contract tests colocated.
issues.suggested_fix: Fold the id-return check into test_emit_writes_event rather than introducing a near-duplicate test. Alternatively, parametrize if you need multiple emit shapes.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The diff is test-only but claims a contract the production code does not implement: `test_emit_returns_event_id` asserts `event_id is not None` against `EventStore.emit()`, which is declared `-> None` at orchestrator/src/orchestrator/event_store.py:97 and never returns a value. The test will fail on the first run, and the `event_id =` capture added to test_emit_writes_event is dead code with the same root cause — the production `emit()` must be updated to return `cursor.lastrowid` (while preserving its fire-and-forget contract on exceptions) for the test to mean what it says. Separately, the diff weakens `test_emit_writes_event` from an exact `len(rows) == 1` to `>= 1` with a task_id filter, despite each test using a fresh `tmp_path` db — this silently drops regression coverage. The new test's assertions are also redundant (`any(...)` + `len > 0`) and don't link `event_id` back to the stored row's `id`, which is the one behavior that would actually be worth testing. Recommend either dropping this diff and re-submitting with a production-side change to `emit()`, or reverting to the old test and adding a proper `row['id'] == event_id` assertion once the contract is real.
